idea-plugin.name: Ergo
idea-plugin.description:
Ergo reveals the concrete errors a Go function can return.

Go functions return the error interface, never a concrete type, so a signature never tells you which errors you might get back. Ergo answers that: it statically traces the data flow through a function and reports every concrete error value that can reach one of its return statements — recursing through the functions it calls, including the standard library.

Hover a Go function or method and the result appears as an "Errors (ergo)" section in the Quick Documentation popup, below the usual documentation. Each finding is one of:

  • sentinel — a package-level error value, e.g. io.EOF;
  • type — a concrete error type, e.g. *os.PathError;
  • constructed — an errors.New or fmt.Errorf error, with its literal message (%w wrapping is followed);
  • unresolved — an error whose origin cannot be determined, such as one returned through an interface method.

Every finding links to the source location the error originates from, so you can jump straight to where it is created.

Requires the Go plugin — bundled in GoLand, or installable in IntelliJ IDEA Ultimate.
